description: Accruals computed based on the difference between accounting earnings and cash flow from operations, scaled by total assets, to reflect non-cash adjustments to earnings
Benford forensic score measuring deviations from expected digit frequency distributions (per Benford’s Law) in financial statement data, indicating possible data manipulation if the deviation is high
A composite model-derived score predicting the likelihood of accounting misstatements by aggregating signals from accruals, performance, and other metrics, tailored to detect probable misconduct
Accruals calculated using the (original) Jones Model, a standard approach to estimate discretionary and non-discretionary accruals, helping flag earnings management risk

Accruals calculated using the Modified Jones Model, which isolates discretionary accruals by assuming only credit sales are discretionary, refining the measurement compared to the standard Jones Model
Beneish M-Score: a statistical score derived to assess the likelihood of earnings manipulation by analyzing key financial ratios and accounting indices

A machine learning-based metric indicating the probability that a company will issue a financial statement restatement, trained mainly on U.S. data to detect restatement risk, which is often associated with subsequent negative stock performance
An overall, equally weighted composite score assessing a company's accounting quality, aggregating metrics from various accounting models in the wolfe_csi framework
Working capital accruals, typically calculated as the accrual component of working capital, scaled by total assets as an indicator of potential earnings manipulation
Working capital accruals estimated via the Yoon model, which incorporates more comprehensive factors (e.g., cash sales, cash/noncash expenses) for improved detection of discretionary accruals
